prompt:
Reliability ticket from the plant-floor ingest team. Our conveyor telemetry goes to Splunk over the HTTP Event Collector through a shell script that curls and hopes — after last month's indexer rolling restart we lost twenty minutes of events that HEC had already 200'd. I want a proper Python sender package, `splunk_hec`, that uses HEC *indexer acknowledgment* so a 200 alone is never treated as delivered. Stdlib only (urllib/http.client are fine), split sensibly across modules (envelope building/validation, transport/retry, the sender itself). test_hec_ack.py is the acceptance contract — run: python3 test_hec_ack.py.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Splunk documentation for HEC — the indexer-acknowledgment page, the event-format page, and the HEC REST endpoint list on help.splunk.com (Splunk's official GitHub connector sources also count as first-party for wire details). Blogs and Stack Overflow do not count as sources. Nail down: the event vs raw endpoints and their envelope rules, how the request channel is passed (header vs query parameter) and what shape a channel ID must have, the exact ack polling protocol on /services/collector/ack and what HEC does to an ackId's status after it first reports true, the response-code table (which codes are retryable), and how compressed bodies are labeled. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees (the ack response key casing is a known sore spot), report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests drive (see docs/contract.json for the pinned shapes):
- HecSender(base_url, token, channel, sleeper, gzip=False) — Authorization: Splunk <token> on every request, never in a URL or an error string; channel must be GUID-shaped or the constructor raises ValueError; sleeper(seconds) is the only way to wait.
- send_events(events) -> ackId: validates every envelope BEFORE any request (only the documented keys; event required; numeric time; fields flat — scalars or arrays of scalars), serializes one compact JSON envelope per event newline-separated, POSTs to the documented JSON-event endpoint with the channel header, and returns the acknowledgment id. A 200 that carries no acknowledgment id means the token has acknowledgment disabled — that's an error, not a success.
- send_raw(text) -> ackId: raw bytes to the documented raw endpoint with the channel as a query parameter.
- gzip=True: compress data POST bodies and label them with the documented header.
- poll_acks(ack_ids) -> dict[int,bool] speaking the documented ack query protocol.
- wait_until_acked(ack_ids, poll_interval, max_polls): polls, sleeping poll_interval between polls only; once an id reports true it must never be queried again (find out why in the docs); unacked ids after max_polls raise HecAckTimeout with .unacked.
- send_events_with_retry(events, max_attempts, backoff): resends the identical batch after an ambiguous transport failure (connection dies before a response) and after the documented retryable statuses, sleeping backoff between attempts; other 4xx raise HecServerError (.status, .code, .text) immediately; transport failures beyond max_attempts re-raise.
- health() -> bool from the documented health endpoint.

Acceptance: python3 test_hec_ack.py — it runs a loopback fake HEC; no real Splunk, no credentials, no time.sleep anywhere in your code. test_hec_ack.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform)
research.interface: HEC REST endpoints /services/collector/{event,raw,ack,health} with indexer acknowledgment (current Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x docs)

official_sources.title: About HTTP Event Collector Indexer Acknowledgment | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the /services/collector/ack endpoint; channel required on every send and ack query (X-Splunk-Request-Channel header or channel query parameter, GUID format); ack query body {"acks":[...]}; ack response {"acks":{"0":true,...}}; HEC deletes an ackId's status after reporting true so re-querying returns false; poll-at-interval and resend-if-unacked guidance

official_sources.title: Format events for HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: envelope keys event/time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields; epoch <sec>.<ms> time format; batching by concatenated/newline-separated envelopes; fields must be flat (no nested objects) and are only indexed via the /event endpoint; raw endpoint requires a channel identifier

official_sources.title: HTTP Event Collector REST API endpoints | Splunk Enterprise
Troubleshoot HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the HEC response code table: 0 Success/200, 6 Invalid data format/400, 9 Server is busy/503, 10 Data channel is missing/400, 14 ACK is disabled/400, 17 HEC is healthy/200, 18 HEC is unhealthy queues are full/503, 26 HEC queue is at capacity/429; guidance that clients must act on response codes

official_sources.title: splunk/fluent-plugin-splunk-hec out_splunk_hec.rb (official Splunk HEC connector source)
official_sources.used_for: the gzip option: official Splunk HEC connector sets Content-Encoding: gzip and gzip-compresses the POST body when gzip_compression is enabled

official_sources.title: splunk/kafka-connect-splunk PostResponse.java (official Splunk HEC client source)
official_sources.used_for: the exact success wire response {"text":"Success","code":0,"ackId":<int>} — in particular the ackId key casing on the wire

research.language_rationale: Our shipping-floor forwarders are Python daemons. Splunk documents HEC as a raw REST interface (the official splunk-sdk-python does not cover HEC indexer acknowledgment), so stdlib urllib against the documented /services/collector endpoints is the credible, dependency-free integration surface; Splunk's own HEC connectors implement the same wire contract.
